text:
There lived a young maiden named Arabella. Arabella lived purely.

One autumn she lost her favourite feather.

She searched everywhere. At last she tracked it.

It was blown into an old church.

Inside, she looked around.

Her feather?

It floated right out over there.

With wings!

Arabella chased it. It danced.

It flew!

Oh no!

She lunged.

 thunk!

It grazed her nose.

Now feathers.

They fluttered.

You go?

Yes?It seems I have been asleep.

Oh yes.

You flew over here.

It wasn't far.

You know you weren't scared?

Oh. No.

There was nothing dangerous.

My family.

No owls. Ever.

They had.

They wanted me.

So they left me here?

Now?

I fly there.

You just fly.


There?

The bell tower?They would talk.

They wanted more stories.

You tell them.

What have you got?

A song. With birds.

You sing?


They listen
